body:
Title: CRA Form NU479 2025 Nunavut credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-tc/5014-tc-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5014-tc/5014-tc-25e.pdf

NU479 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 0.02
Value: 1500
Value: 60000
Value: 255.12
Value: 100
Value: 900
Value: 0.50
Value: 500

CRA Form NU479 2025 calculates Nunavut refundable credits. Lines 1 to 5 calculate adjusted net income by subtracting lines 24400 and 25000 from line 23600, flooring at zero, then subtracting foreign income from line 25600 that is exempt under a tax treaty and flooring again. Lines 6 to 8 calculate the basic credit by multiplying adjusted net income by 2% and capping the credit at $1,500. Lines 9 to 13 calculate the cost of living supplement for single parents: the supplement applies if the taxpayer was a single parent at any time in 2025 and adjusted net income is more than $60,000, multiplies income above $60,000 by 2%, and caps the supplement at $255.12. Line 14 adds the basic credit and supplement.

The political contribution chart for line 16 gives the contribution amount itself for contributions of $100 or less, gives $500 for contributions of $900 or more, and otherwise subtracts $100, multiplies the excess by 50%, adds $100, and caps the line 16 amount at $500. Line 19 subtracts line 14 from line 66 of Form NU428 and floors the result at zero. Line 20 is the lesser of line 16 and line 19. Line 21 adds line 14 and line 20 and enters Nunavut credits on T1 line 47900.
heading: CRA Form NU479 2025 Nunavut Credits
